answer:
After César sprayed graffiti depicting a dead dog (referencing the murder of Ellen Henry’s dog, Jack), he was **shot with a morphine-filled syringe attached to an arrow**, causing him to pass out. When he woke up, he discovered **one of his fingers had been cut off**, with the letters **"R U DUN"** tattooed on it as a warning. This act marked the escalation of Garfield’s vigilante justice against the K.O.S. gang.

\boxed{César was shot with a morphine-tipped arrow, woke up to find one of his fingers cut off with "R U DUN" tattooed on it, and later died in a Russian roulette duel with Garfield.}
